requirements.statement: Jido shall provide action surfaces for validated state transformation and built-in control, lifecycle, status, and scheduling behaviors.
Jido shall treat `cmd/2` and `cmd/3` as pure decision functions that return an updated agent together with explicit effects.
Jido shall let agent modules declare schema-backed state, defaults, and hooks through `use Jido.Agent`.
Jido shall document and expose configuration defaults suitable for local development and deployment environments.
Jido shall provide discovery and introspection APIs for actions, sensors, and related runtime metadata.
Jido shall provide runtime debugging facilities for inspecting recent execution and runtime state.
Jido shall expose structured public error contracts across actions, directives, and signal processing.
Jido shall document integration paths for Phoenix and Ash-based applications.
Jido shall document how maintainers migrate from the 1.x runtime model to the current instance-scoped runtime model.
Jido shall support partition as a logical namespace boundary for runtimes in a shared instance.
Jido shall propagate partition context through pod membership, hierarchy, persistence, and runtime lookup paths by default.
Jido shall treat Pods as the durable tenant or workspace unit in the shared-instance tenancy model.
Jido shall emit structured observability and telemetry events for agent and pod execution.
Jido shall preserve trace and span context across runtime execution and signal flow.
Jido shall support plugin lifecycle hooks for initialization, restore, checkpoint, and runtime middleware behavior.
Jido shall let plugins mount isolated state, actions, routes, and configuration onto agents.
Jido shall let sensors translate external events into routed Jido signals.
Jido shall model a Pod as a durable topology snapshot for a named group of agents.
Jido shall support live add and remove mutation of pod topology while keeping the topology itself durable.
Jido shall support nested pod topologies as part of the durable group model.
Jido shall reconcile eager pod members automatically and let lazy members start on demand through ensure operations.
Jido shall host live agents inside AgentServer processes that execute directives, expose runtime status, and isolate agent state from transport concerns.
Jido shall provide event-driven await APIs for async coordination instead of relying on sleep-based polling.
Jido shall support tracked child hierarchies, adoption, explicit orphan policies, and child stop control within the runtime.
Jido shall provide durable keyed instance-management patterns for starting, locating, hibernating, and thawing named runtimes.
Jido shall support hibernate and thaw flows for durable keyed runtimes.
Jido shall support pluggable storage backends for persisted runtime checkpoints and related state.
Jido shall represent external effects as directives executed by the runtime rather than hidden mutations inside pure agent code.
Jido shall support scheduling, cron delivery, and cancellation through explicit directive and runtime flows.
Jido shall route CloudEvents-style signals to agent behavior through typed routing rules instead of ad hoc process messages.
Jido shall keep non-trivial state mutation inside strategy-applied state operations instead of treating them as external directives.
Jido shall provide a custom strategy interface for specialized execution patterns.
Jido shall provide a direct strategy for single-turn action execution without changing the purity of agent logic.
Jido shall provide an FSM strategy for multi-state execution flows driven by explicit machine state.
Jido shall let strategies own the translation between pure agent results, internal state operations, and runtime directives.
Jido shall provide test support for isolated instances and event-driven async assertions without sleep-based polling.
Jido shall keep runnable example and docs-example suites that exercise core public patterns.
Jido shall document testing patterns for pure logic and runtime integration.
Jido shall provide a persistent identity capability for durable agent identity and profile state.
Jido shall provide a persistent memory capability for durable agent memory state.
Jido shall provide a persistent thread capability for structured conversational or event journals.
Jido shall provide install and generator tooling that helps applications adopt the framework.
Jido shall provide worker-pool patterns for reusable pooled agent execution.
Jido shall provide an Elixir framework for autonomous agents, workflows, and multi-agent systems.
Jido shall treat agent modules as pure decision units whose core contract is cmd/2 returning an updated agent together with directives.
Jido shall keep runtime side effects explicit through directives and AgentServer runtime modules instead of hiding effects inside agent state transitions.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that tells agents to start a session with mix spec.prime before editing current truth.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.
When indexing and validation run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.

meta.summary: Jido agents are schema-backed pure decision units with explicit action surfaces.
Jido exposes configuration defaults and discovery APIs for actions, sensors, and runtime metadata.
Jido provides runtime debugging tools and structured public error contracts.
Jido documents framework integrations and the migration path from the 1.x runtime model.
Jido supports shared-instance logical multi-tenancy through partitions and pod-first tenancy boundaries.
Jido exposes production observability through structured telemetry, observation, and tracing.
Jido is an Elixir framework for autonomous agent systems with pure agent logic and explicit runtime effects.
Jido composes capabilities through plugins and ingests external events through sensors.
Jido Pods provide durable topology, activation, nesting, and live mutation for grouped agents.
Jido runtime lifecycle is hosted by AgentServer and coordinated through hierarchy, await, and instance-management APIs.
Jido persists durable keyed runtimes through hibernate/thaw flows and pluggable storage backends.
Jido routes signals, models side effects as directives, and keeps state operations internal to strategy execution.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs and generated Spec Led state.
Strategies control how pure agent logic becomes state transitions and directives at runtime.
Jido provides isolation-friendly test support and runnable examples for core public flows.
Jido provides persistent thread, memory, and identity capabilities as structured plugins.
Jido provides worker-pool execution patterns and install or generator tooling for adoption.
